one_line_thesis: Star Holdings trades ~54% below Q1 2026 book (~$19.88/sh) as a legacy iStar wind-down with a large Safehold stake; base case 10.4% per year if ~75% of book is returned over five years — watch pending related-party review.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Star Holdings is a legacy asset monetization vehicle with a marked Safehold stake, shrinking legacy portfolio, and active repurchases. Book value per share is about $19.88 at March 31, 2026; the quote near $9.15 is roughly 54% below book with a margin loan secured by the Safe shares but no operating moat. This is not a compounder: ten-year free-cash-flow math is the wrong primary lens. Our base case uses a five-year capital-return path to realize roughly 75% of book (~$15 per share) through asset sales, Safe dividends, and buybacks, implying about 10.4% per year from today's price. Downside...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Realization is modeled through the five-year wind-down horizon, with specific sale and repayment timing remaining uncertain.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: The recovery range applies haircuts to the disclosed loans, AFS securities, land, and properties; the base case relies on repayment and land-sale evidence while zero-carry claims receive no value here.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: No incremental development capital is attributed to this monetizing bucket; sale costs and wind-down friction are deducted separately in wind_down_fee_and_friction_reserve.

components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
components.falsifier: Sale/repayment proceeds imply recovery below $3.50/sh on this bucket.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Land and operating-real-estate realization is modeled over the five-year wind-down horizon; no immediate sale of the remaining operating real estate is assumed.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: The base case gives partial credit to carrying value because 2023 through Q1 2026 land and lot sales exceeded cost of sales, while the low case allows slow or distressed realization and the high case approaches carrying.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: The component is valued after its carrying-value haircut; no separate development budget is added, and fee and sale-friction deductions remain in wind_down_fee_and_friction_reserve.

components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
components.falsifier: Monetization clears below $4/sh after costs on this residual bucket.

probability_and_timing.timing_basis: Any recovery is treated as a contingent result within the five-year wind-down horizon, not as current cash or a contracted asset sale.
probability_and_timing.probability_basis: The low case assigns zero, the base assigns only $0.20 per share, and the high case requires incremental recovery from zero-carry claims or entitlement surplus beyond Magnolia/Asbury carrying cases.
probability_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: No incremental owner-funded development spend is credited to this option; any continuing holding, entitlement, management, or sale costs are captured in the existing operating and wind-down reserve components.

components.tax_and_realization_adjustments: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
components.falsifier: No surplus to carrying and zero-carry assets remain worthless.

economic_value.accounting_reference: STHO Q1 2026 Form 10-Q; FY2025 Form 10-K; 8-K 2026-04-01 Asbury deconsolidation.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of STHO, including every identified asset, liability, and option in the wind-down vehicle.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: SAFE marked gross; Margin Loan and Safe Credit in senior_debt; monetizing vs Magnolia/Asbury residual split to avoid double count.

valuation_proof.evidence: Q1 2026 Form 10-Q (STHO/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260508_rpt20260331_acc0001953366_26_000010.htm); proof base 18.478/sh via net_asset_value@1.0.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.falsifier: SAFE fails to re-rate with lower rates, or forced sale/collateral path nets below the low case.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: MD&A monetizing loans, AFS securities, land, other properties
valuation_proof.evidence: Q1 2026 Form 10-Q (STHO/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260508_rpt20260331_acc0001953366_26_000010.htm); proof base 5.3012/sh via probability_weighted_catalyst_nav@1.0.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Sale/repayment proceeds imply recovery below $3.50/sh on this bucket.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Realization is modeled through the five-year wind-down horizon, with specific sale and repayment timing remaining uncertain.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: The recovery range applies haircuts to the disclosed loans, AFS securities, land, and properties; the base case relies on repayment and land-sale evidence while zero-carry claims receive no value here.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: No incremental development capital is attributed to this monetizing bucket; sale costs and wind-down friction are deducted separately in wind_down_fee_and_friction_reserve.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Magnolia Green and residual Asbury land / operating real estate
valuation_proof.evidence: Q1 2026 Form 10-Q (STHO/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-Q_20260508_rpt20260331_acc0001953366_26_000010.htm); proof base 7.5012/sh via probability_weighted_catalyst_nav@1.0.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Low/base/high incorporate ownership, financing, tax, and realization adjustments in component evidence.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Monetization clears below $4/sh after costs on this residual bucket.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Land and operating-real-estate realization is modeled over the five-year wind-down horizon; no immediate sale of the remaining operating real estate is assumed.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: The base case gives partial credit to carrying value because 2023 through Q1 2026 land and lot sales exceeded cost of sales, while the low case allows slow or distressed realization and the high case approaches carrying.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: The component is valued after its carrying-value haircut; no separate development budget is added, and fee and sale-friction deductions remain in wind_down_fee_and_friction_reserve.

timeline.label: Q1 2026 results: net loss driven by non-cash SAFE mark; Surfhouse mezz repayment noted in MD&A.
Q1 2026: ~13.5M Safehold shares marked at $183M ($13.53/SAFE); stockholders' equity ~$240M (~$19.88/sh).
Asbury Park seller-financed venture deconsolidated after $10.6M mezz repayment; simplified balance sheet and improved net cash flexibility.
Continued Magnolia Green and Asbury land/lot sales above cost of sales; legacy monetizing portfolio shrinks.
Management fee stepped down to $10M annual term; wind-down fee structure continues toward 2% GBV ex-SAFE after 2027-03-31.
Margin loan and Safe Credit facilities amended; collateral terms eased per Note 9 disclosure.
Share repurchase program active; FY2025 ~$8M buybacks and ongoing Q1 2026 repurchases.
Star Holdings spun from iStar/Safehold merger with legacy non-ground-lease assets and large SAFE stake; wind-down mandate.

industry.structure: Star Holdings is a single-asset-pool wind-down REIT holdco, not an operating franchise; value is realization of legacy iStar loans, land, and a minority Safehold stake.
industry.share_shift: Legacy iStar assets monetize while SAFE mark moves with rate-sensitive ground-lease equities; no operating market share.
industry.trend: Post-spin orphan discount persists; related-party Safehold management and margin loan on SAFE shares add complexity versus pure NAV vehicles.

top_claims.claim: Revenue growth moved from +14.5% to +43.2% YoY for two consecutive quarters. Source: SEC XBRL quarterly filings.

Star Holdings is a legacy asset monetization vehicle with a marked Safehold stake, shrinking legacy portfolio, and active repurchases. Book value per share is about $19.88 at March 31, 2026; the quote near $9.15 is roughly 54% below book with a margin loan secured by the Safe shares but no operating moat. This is not a
*Primary risk: Legacy assets (Asbury, Magnolia Green) or the Safehold stake sell below carrying values, eroding book faster than repurchases shrink the share count.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Legacy assets (Asbury, Magnolia Green) or the Safehold stake sell below carrying values, eroding book faster than repurchases shrink the share count.
Inversion: What if they fail? Prolonged low price with rising management fees and margin-loan constraints could trap value inside the vehicle.
SAFE mark declines force margin-loan prepayments or restrict share sales (10-K risk factors).
